parsed_evaluation.task_summary: Compute the light travel time from Earth to Mars using their average distances from the Sun and show step-by-step calculations.

rubrics.description: Compute Earth–Mars separation from average orbital distances.
rubrics.criterion: The execution trace shows Earth at ~1 AU and Mars at ~1.52–1.53 AU (or their km equivalents) and computes the separation as the difference (~0.51–0.54 AU or ~7.6e7–8.1e7 km).

rubrics.description: Apply the speed of light with consistent units to get time in seconds.
rubrics.criterion: The agent uses either c ≈ 299,792 km/s with a km distance, or 1 AU ≈ 499 s with an AU distance, and computes a travel time of approximately 258–265 seconds.

rubrics.description: Convert to minutes and present step-by-step intermediate values.
rubrics.criterion: The final output states the time in minutes (~4.3–4.4 minutes) and includes intermediate numeric values for the Earth–Mars separation and the time in seconds before the minutes conversion.

task_objective: How many minutes does it take light to travel from Earth to Mars (at their average distances from the Sun)? Show each step.
